violations.claim: Ordering constraint violation: line-number ordering is used as a call-graph proxy.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 70
; Ordering constraint violation: line-number ordering is used as a call-graph proxy.
; An earlier-line function is not necessarily a caller of the later-line function.
(declare-const caller_line Int)
(declare-const contract_line Int)
(declare-const same_file Int)              ; 1 = caller.file === contract.file
(declare-const actually_calls_contract Int); 1 = caller truly invokes contract's function
(declare-const check_emitted Int)          ; 1 = AxiomCheck is pushed to array
; Code transition: filter passes when same_file=1 AND caller_line < contract_line
(assert (= same_file 1))
(assert (< caller_line contract_line))
; Code transition: no call-graph lookup performed — filter is purely positional
(assert (= actually_calls_contract 0))
; Code transition: filter passes → check is pushed regardless of actual call relationship
(assert (= check_emitted 1))
; Violation: a P1 check is generated for a non-caller pair
(assert (= actually_calls_contract 0))
(assert (= check_emitted 1))
(check-sat)
